name: ruby-naught
title: ruby-naught
summary: Naught is a toolkit for building Null Objects
description:
Naught is a toolkit for building Null Objects
Null Objects can make your code more confident (Confident Ruby is a book
by Avdi Grimm on Patterns for Joyful Coding).
.
While you don't *need* a Null Object library, this one offers some
conveniences you probably won't find elsewhere.
.
Learn more about this library from /usr/share/ruby-naught/README.markdown
(It has many examples).
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ruby-naught because Naught is a toolkit for building Null Objects.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use ruby-naught as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ruby-naught with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ruby-naught --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
